summary: End-to-end validation of (a) the from_inline_pem OCI config helper via an OCI control-plane call, and (b) the AIDP `aidataplatform` ORACLE_ALH format handler reading a real Oracle table on the new ALHTEST instance.
what_validated: from_inline_pem() builds an OCI config dict from inline PEM (no FUSE files) — verified by oci.object_storage.list_buckets returning 8 buckets in compartment AIDP_TPC-DS_Ahmed
spark.read.format('aidataplatform').option('type', 'ORACLE_ALH') format handler is registered on the AIDP `tpcds` cluster and resolves successfully
Required ORACLE_ALH options discovered: tns, wallet.content (base64 ZIP), wallet.password, user.name, password, schema, table
Read returned 5 rows with correct schema (ID NUMBER, LABEL VARCHAR2, TS TIMESTAMP)
key_lessons: ORACLE_ALH expects `tns` not `connection.identifier` (initial attempt with connection.identifier raised DATA_ACCESS_LAYER_0001).
ORACLE_ALH expects `schema` + `table` separately (initial attempt with `table=schema.table` raised DATA_ACCESS_LAYER_0014).
wallet.content takes the whole wallet.zip base64-encoded — not a path.
skill_correctness: scripts/oracle_ai_data_platform_connectors/auth/oci_config.py from_inline_pem() validated. The aidataplatform ORACLE_ALH option shape now documented in the skill.
test_database_disposition: ALHTEST instance created in compartment AIDP_TPC-DS_Ahmed. Both rows 2+3 PASS — instance can now be terminated to stop billing.
